title: Pectis leptocephala
intro: Pectis leptocephala, also known as Mexican daisy or coyota, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Pectis leptocephala include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Pectis leptocephala is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Pectis leptocephala is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places Pectis leptocephala among the flowering plants, specifically within the tribe Pectideae. Further classification includes the subfamily Vernonioideae, which comprises several genera of flowering plants. Pectis leptocephala is a native species to the Americas, adding to its unique classification within the plant kingdom. It is a member of a diverse group of plants that exhibit varying growth habits and characteristics.
uses: Pectis leptocephala is a plant species traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico and other parts of Central America. Its leaves and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is also used to make a tea that is said to have anti-inflammatory properties. In addition, Pectis leptocephala is believed to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Pectis leptocephala for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Pectis leptocephala, also known as Indian paintbrush or firecracker plant, is not typically associated with any serious side effects.
